response.justification: Candidate has ~13 years of broad software experience but the role requires deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation, which the resume does not demonstrate. The only C/C++ experience is a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; the last decade has been spent on JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang in web, fintech, and martech domains. There is no evidence of MQTT, IEC 62443/SDL, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or message-broker-based high-performance C++ systems, making this a poor match despite seniority and solid engineering fundamentals.
strengths: 13+ years of professional engineering experience across backend, embedded-adjacent, and platform work
Strong software craftsmanship background — TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and architectural ownership
Some low-latency, high-throughput systems exposure (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic micro-services at millions of req/s)
concerns: Only ~6 months of actual C/C++ experience, over a decade ago; no recent STL, multi-threaded, or modern C++ work shown
No evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, or industrial protocols required by the role
Recent stack is JS/TS/Node and DLang — significant ramp-up needed to meet senior C++ expectations

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) or SDL background
Strong general engineering seniority and mentoring track record
